mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending power, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of instances when UNRATE crosses above 8.0%, or out-of-sample accuracy drops below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad contraction in consumer demand, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative returns with a logit coefficient significance of p < 0.05, or predictive R-squared remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp month-over-month contractions in industrial production reflect immediate supply-side bottlenecks and inventory drawdowns, triggering short-term negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of INDPRO contraction months, or the signal loses predictive power during recovery phases (IC < 0).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price inflation above 3% annually forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, increasing discount rates and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3%, or the correlation between inflation and returns turns positive over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total employment and rising unemployment indicates labor market slack and underemployment, which suppresses wage growth and consumer confidence, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence shows no statistical relationship with 20-day returns (p > 0.10), or predictive accuracy falls below chance levels.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects expanding economic activity and corporate earnings potential, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a t-statistic > 1.96, or the signal reverses direction during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly industrial production drop exceeding 0.5% signals severe manufacturing weakness and impending earnings downgrades, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 20d returns in > 55% of months where INDPRO falls > 0.5%, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.
